ISSUING COMMISSION TO TAKE DEPOSITION OUT OF STATE UNDER CCP 2026 FILED BY DEFENDANT MCCARTHY, ANGUS F.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANGUS MCCARTHY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT IS AN UNENFORCEABLE AGREEMENT TO AGREE. PLAINTIFF CAN PROFFER PAROL EVIDENCE REGARDING THE PARTIES' PRIOR COURSE OF DEALING TO ATTEMPT TO ESTABLISH AN ENFORCEABLE CONTRACT. "IN THE ABSENCE OF ANY AGREEMENT AS TO COMPENSATION A REASONABLE COMMISSION MAY PROPERLY BE INFERRED, OR WHERE THERE HAS BEEN A PAROL AGREEMENT AS TO COMPENSATION[,] EVIDENCE THEREOF IS ADMISSIBLE TO ESTABLISH THE AGREED AMOUNT." FRIDDLE V. EPSTEIN (1993) 16 CAL.APP.4TH 1649, 1656. THE CONTRACT IS SUPPORTED BY ADEQUATE CONSIDERATION. AFTER THE PARTIES EXECUTED THE CONTRACT, PLAINTIFF DID ATTEMPT TO LOCATE NEW DEVELOPMENTS FOR DEFENDANT. IN ADDITION, "[I]N A BILATERAL CONTRACT, THE PROMISE OF ONE PARTY IS CONSIDERATION FOR THAT OF ANOTHER. THE GENERAL RULE, SUBJECT TO CERTAIN EXCEPTIONS, IS THAT ANY VALID PROMISE, WHETHER ABSOLUTE OR CONDITIONAL, IS SUFFICIENT CONSIDERATION FOR ANOTHER PROMISE." WITKIN, SUMMARY OF CA LAW (10TH ED. 2005 ) CONTRACTS 212 AT P. 217. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE CONTRACT EXPIRED. THE CONTRACT PROVIDES THAT PLAINTIFF COULD LIST THE PROPERTIES "ONE YEAR FROM COMPLETION OF ANY PROJECT." THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT IN HIRING OTHER REAL ESTATE BROKERS EXCUSED PLAINTIFF'S PERFORMANCE. WITH REGARD TO THE FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING SCIENTER AND PLAINTIFF'S DETRIMENTAL RELIANCE. THE ECONOMIC LOSS RULE DOES NOT APPLY TO THIS CASE. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER PLAINTIFF CAN RECOVER PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE COURT REJECTS THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ARGUMENT BECAUSE IT WAS RAISED FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THE REPLY BRIEF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.
